NOT NULL,\n160\t    result_id    TEXT,\n161\t    topic_id     TEXT,\n162\t    timestamp    INTEGER NOT NULL,\n163\t    data         TEXT NOT NULL,  -- JSON: signal-specific data\n164\t    surface_type TEXT,\n165\t    processed    INTEGER DEFAULT 0 CHECK(processed IN (0, 1)),\n166\t    processed_at INTEGER,\n167\t    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n168\t    FOREIGN KEY (result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,\n169\t    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n170\t);\n171\t\n172\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_session ON feedback_signals(session_id);\n173\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_type ON feedback_signals(signal_type);\n174\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_processed ON feedback_signals(processed);\n175\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_signals_result ON feedback_signals(result_id);\n176\t\n177\t-- Per-stage latency capture for every dispatch (Latency Budget & Instrumentation).\n178\t-- Keyed by the intent *thread* id (routed_intent.intent_id — the same id the\n179\t-- fetch/synthesize/escalate strands and the results row use), NOT the\n180\t-- intents.id the caller mints in create_intent(). router_ms is shared across\n181\t-- every intent thread from the same utterance; escalate_ms is NULL for hot-path\n182\t-- dispatches; stt_ms/first_render_ms are client-reported and NULL when the\n183\t-- client never reports them. synthesize_first_token_ms is NULL until the\n184\t-- synthesize strand streams (the non-streaming call_simple path can't measure\n185\t-- first token separately) — see src/instrument/timings.py.\n186\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dispatch_timings (\n187\t    intent_id                 TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n188\t    router_ms                 INTEGER,\n189\t    fetch_first_source_ms     INTEGER,\n190\t    fetch_total_ms            INTEGER,\n191\t    synthesize_first_token_ms INTEGER,\n192\t    synthesize_total_ms       INTEGER,\n193\t    escalate_ms               INTEGER,\n194\t    sse_emit_ms               INTEGER,\n195\t    stt_ms                    INTEGER,\n196\t    first_render_ms           INTEGER,\n197\t    created_at                INTEGER NOT NULL\n198\t);\n199\t\n200\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dispatch_timings_created ON dispatch_timings(created_at);\n201\t\n202\t-- Bead watch table: circuit breaker tracking for async beads\n203\t-- Persistence layer for the async-path circuit breaker (plan §10 The Async Path).\n204\t-- Tracks open beads watched by the BeadWatcher, recording refusal counts,\n205\t-- last refusal reasons, SLA deadlines, and fencing state. All state is persisted\n206\t-- so a watcher restart loses nothing -- breaker state lives in the database,\n207\t-- not only in memory.\n208\tCREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bead_watch (\n209\t    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,  -- References bead ID (intents.bead_ref)\n210\t    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,  -- Number of REFUSED: comments seen\n211\t    last_refusal_reason TEXT,  -- Most recent refusal reason\n212\t    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,  -- Timestamp of most recent refusal\n213\t    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,  -- Latest comment index processed (-1 = none)\n214\t    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,  -- Unix timestamp when SLA expires\n215\t    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when SLA was flagged (NULL if not flagged)\n216\t    fenced_at          INTEGER,  -- Timestamp when bead was fenced to status=blocked (NULL if not fenced)\n217\t    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL  -- When this watch row was created\n218\t);\n219\t\n220\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch(sla_deadline);\n221\tCREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch(fenced_at);\n222\t\"\"\"\n223\t\n224\t# The set of dispatch_timings timing columns record_dispatch_timings() may set.\n225\t# intent_id/created_at are handled explicitly (PK / insert-only timestamp).\n226\tDISPATCH_TIMING_COLUMNS = (\n227\t    \"router_ms\",\n228\t    \"fetch_first_source_ms\",\n229\t    \"fetch_total_ms\",\n230\t    \"synthesize_first_token_ms\",\n231\t    \"synthesize_total_ms\",\n232\t    \"escalate_ms\",\n233\t    \"sse_emit_ms\",\n234\t    \"stt_ms\",\n235\t    \"first_render_ms\",\n236\t)\n237\t\n238\t# Valid intent status values (plan §10 The Async Path: stuck/failed added for circuit breaker)\n239\tINTENT_STATUSES = ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')\n240\t\n241\t# Default SLA hours per intent type (plan §10 The Async Path: Visible Aging)\n242\t# task-profile: 6h (async bead-backed tasks)\n243\t# hot-path intents: 30s (budget is 3s, flag at 10x for aged pending)\n244\tDEFAULT_SLA_HOURS: dict[str, float] = {\n245\t    \"task-profile\": 6.0,  # 6 hours for async bead-backed tasks\n246\t    \"status\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path status intents\n247\t    \"action\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path action intents\n248\t    \"lookup\": 0.008,     # 30 seconds for hot-path lookup intents\n249\t    \"brainstorm\": 0.5,   # 30 minutes for brainstorm (may need user iteration)\n250\t    \"reminder\": 24.0,    # 24 hours for reminders\n251\t}\n252\t\n253\t# Circuit breaker thresholds (plan §10 The Async Path)\n254\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_REFUSAL_THRESHOLD = 3  # Fence after N refusals\n255\tCIRCUIT_BREAKER_AGE_THRESHOLD_HOURS = 24.0  # Fence after N hours without progress\n256\t\n257\t\n258\tclass SessionStore:\n259\t    \"\"\"Session store with WAL mode for concurrent access.\"\"\"\n260\t\n261\t    def __init__(self, db_path: Path):\n262\t        self.db_path = db_path\n263\t\n264\t    async def initialize(self) -> None:\n265\t        \"\"\"Initialize database with schema and WAL mode.\"\"\"\n266\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n267\t            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n268\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n269\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n270\t\n271\t            # Create schema\n272\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n273\t            await db.commit()\n274\t\n275\t            # Additive migrations: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS above creates new\n276\t            # columns only for freshly-made DBs; an existing data/session.db\n277\t            # (e.g. the live Hetzner box) keeps its old column set and must be\n278\t            # ALTERed. Each statement is guarded by a PRAGMA table_info probe so\n279\t            # it is idempotent and never re-runs once the column exists.\n280\t            await self._migrate_additive_columns(db)\n281\t\n282\t    @staticmethod\n283\t    async def _migrate_additive_columns(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n284\t        \"\"\"Idempotently add columns introduced after the initial schema.\"\"\"\n285\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(results)\") as cur:\n286\t            result_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n287\t        if \"result_type\" not in result_cols:\n288\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN result_type TEXT\")\n289\t        if \"card_fallback\" not in result_cols:\n290\t            await db.execute(\n291\t                \"ALTER TABLE results ADD COLUMN card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0\"\n292\t            )\n293\t\n294\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n295\t            intent_cols = {row[1] for row in await cur.fetchall()}\n296\t        if \"lookup_kind\" not in intent_cols:\n297\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents ADD COLUMN lookup_kind TEXT\")\n298\t\n299\t        # Migrate intents.status CHECK constraint to include 'stuck' and 'failed'\n300\t        # SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we need to recreate the table\n301\t        # First, check if the table still has the old constraint (without stuck/failed)\n302\t        await SessionStore._migrate_intents_status_enum(db)\n303\t\n304\t        # Migrate results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\n305\t        await SessionStore._migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db)\n306\t\n307\t        # Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\n308\t        await SessionStore._migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db)\n309\t\n310\t        await db.commit()\n311\t\n312\t    @staticmethod\n313\t    async def _migrate_intents_status_enum(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n314\t        \"\"\"Migrate intents table to support 'stuck' and 'failed' status values.\n315\t\n316\t        Plan §10 The Async Path: extend intents status enum with stuck/failed.\n317\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.\n318\t        \"\"\"\n319\t        # Check if migration is needed by looking for 'stuck' in the schema\n320\t        async with db.execute(\"PRAGMA table_info(intents)\") as cur:\n321\t            cols = await cur.fetchall()\n322\t            status_col = None\n323\t            for col in cols:\n324\t                if col[1] == \"status\":\n325\t                    status_col = col\n326\t                    break\n327\t\n328\t        # If no status column or already has stuck/failed, skip migration\n329\t        if not status_col:\n330\t            return\n331\t\n332\t        # Check if the CHECK constraint already includes stuck/failed\n333\t        # by inspecting the SQL for the intents table\n334\t        async with db.execute(\n335\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='intents'\"\n336\t        ) as cur:\n337\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n338\t            if table_sql and (\"'stuck'\" in table_sql[0] or \"'failed'\" in table_sql[0]):\n339\t                # Already migrated\n340\t                return\n341\t\n342\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new constraint\n343\t        logger.info(\"Migrating intents.status to include 'stuck' and 'failed' values\")\n344\t\n345\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n346\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n347\t\n348\t        try:\n349\t            # Create new table with updated constraint\n350\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n351\t                CREATE TABLE intents_new (\n352\t                    id           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n353\t                    utterance_id TEXT NOT NULL,\n354\t                    session_id   TEXT NOT NULL,\n355\t                    topic_id     TEXT,\n356\t                    project_slug TEXT,\n357\t                    intent_type  TEXT NOT NULL,\n358\t                    lookup_kind  TEXT,\n359\t                    status       TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(status IN ('pending', 'dispatched', 'resolved', 'cancelled', 'stuck', 'failed')) DEFAULT 'pending',\n360\t                    bead_ref     TEXT,\n361\t                    created_at   INTEGER NOT NULL,\n362\t                    resolved_at  INTEGER,\n363\t                    FOREIGN KEY (utterance_id) REFERENCES utterances(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n364\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n365\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n366\t                )\n367\t            \"\"\")\n368\t\n369\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n370\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n371\t                INSERT INTO intents_new\n372\t                SELECT id, utterance_id, session_id, topic_id, project_slug,\n373\t                       intent_type, lookup_kind, status, bead_ref, created_at, resolved_at\n374\t                FROM intents\n375\t            \"\"\")\n376\t\n377\t            # Recreate indexes\n378\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_session\")\n379\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_topic\")\n380\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_intents_status\")\n381\t\n382\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_session ON intents_new(session_id)\")\n383\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_topic ON intents_new(topic_id)\")\n384\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_intents_status ON intents_new(status)\")\n385\t\n386\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n387\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE intents\")\n388\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE intents_new RENAME TO intents\")\n389\t\n390\t            await db.commit()\n391\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: intents.status now supports 'stuck' and 'failed'\")\n392\t\n393\t        except Exception as e:\n394\t            await db.rollback()\n395\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate intents.status: {e}\")\n396\t            raise\n397\t\n398\t    @staticmethod\n399\t    async def _migrate_results_intent_id_nullable(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n400\t        \"\"\"Migrate results table to allow NULL intent_id for monitoring-originated results.\n401\t\n402\t        Plan §10 Bead Watcher: monitoring-originated results have intent_id=NULL.\n403\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN to drop NOT NULL, so we recreate the table.\n404\t        \"\"\"\n405\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the results table schema\n406\t        async with db.execute(\n407\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='results'\"\n408\t        ) as cur:\n409\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n410\t            if not table_sql:\n411\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n412\t\n413\t            # If intent_id is already nullable (NOT NULL not present), skip migration\n414\t            if \"intent_id   TEXT,\" in table_sql[0] or \"intent_id TEXT,\" in table_sql[0]:\n415\t                if \"NOT NULL\" not in table_sql[0].split(\"intent_id\")[1].split(\",\")[0]:\n416\t                    # Already migrated\n417\t                    return\n418\t\n419\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with nullable intent_id\n420\t        logger.info(\"Migrating results.intent_id to allow NULL for monitoring-originated results\")\n421\t\n422\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n423\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n424\t\n425\t        try:\n426\t            # Create new table with nullable intent_id\n427\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n428\t                CREATE TABLE results_new (\n429\t                    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n430\t                    intent_id   TEXT,\n431\t                    topic_id    TEXT NOT NULL,\n432\t                    session_id  TEXT NOT NULL,\n433\t                    summary     TEXT NOT NULL,\n434\t                    data        TEXT NOT NULL,\n435\t                    urgency     TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(urgency IN ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')) DEFAULT 'normal',\n436\t                    result_type TEXT,\n437\t                    card_fallback INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 CHECK(card_fallback IN (0, 1)),\n438\t                    created_at  INTEGER NOT NULL,\n439\t                    surfaced_at INTEGER,\n440\t                    acked_at    INTEGER,\n441\t                    previous_result_id TEXT,\n442\t                    diff_summary TEXT,\n443\t                    diff_data    TEXT,\n444\t                    FOREIGN KEY (intent_id) REFERENCES intents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n445\t                    FOREIGN KEY (session_id) REFERENCES sessions(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n446\t                    FOREIGN KEY (topic_id) REFERENCES topics(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,\n447\t                    FOREIGN KEY (previous_result_id) REFERENCES results(id) ON DELETE SET NULL\n448\t                )\n449\t            \"\"\")\n450\t\n451\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n452\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n453\t                INSERT INTO results_new\n454\t                SELECT * FROM results\n455\t            \"\"\")\n456\t\n457\t            # Recreate indexes\n458\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_session\")\n459\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_topic\")\n460\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_created\")\n461\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_results_previous\")\n462\t\n463\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_session ON results_new(session_id)\")\n464\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_topic ON results_new(topic_id)\")\n465\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_created ON results_new(created_at)\")\n466\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_results_previous ON results_new(previous_result_id)\")\n467\t\n468\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n469\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE results\")\n470\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE results_new RENAME TO results\")\n471\t\n472\t            await db.commit()\n473\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: results.intent_id now allows NULL\")\n474\t        except Exception as e:\n475\t            await db.rollback()\n476\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate results.intent_id: {e}\")\n477\t            raise\n478\t\n479\t    @staticmethod\n480\t    async def _migrate_bead_watch_comment_high_water(db: aiosqlite.Connection) -> None:\n481\t        \"\"\"Migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1.\n482\t\n483\t        The initial schema had DEFAULT 0, which meant the first comment (index 0)\n484\t        was skipped on the first check. With DEFAULT -1, no comments are skipped\n485\t        initially, and only comments with index > comment_high_water are processed.\n486\t        SQLite doesn't support ALTER COLUMN DEFAULT, so we recreate the table.\n487\t        \"\"\"\n488\t        # Check if migration is needed by inspecting the bead_watch table schema\n489\t        async with db.execute(\n490\t            \"SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='bead_watch'\"\n491\t        ) as cur:\n492\t            table_sql = await cur.fetchone()\n493\t            if not table_sql:\n494\t                return  # Table doesn't exist yet\n495\t\n496\t            # If comment_high_water already has DEFAULT -1, skip migration\n497\t            if \"DEFAULT -1\" in table_sql[0]:\n498\t                # Already migrated\n499\t                return\n500\t\n501\t        # Migration needed: recreate table with new default\n502\t        logger.info(\"Migrating bead_watch.comment_high_water default from 0 to -1\")\n503\t\n504\t        # Begin transaction for data migration\n505\t        await db.execute(\"BEGIN IMMEDIATE TRANSACTION\")\n506\t\n507\t        try:\n508\t            # Create new table with DEFAULT -1\n509\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n510\t                CREATE TABLE bead_watch_new (\n511\t                    bead_ref           TEXT PRIMARY KEY,\n512\t                    refusal_count      INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,\n513\t                    last_refusal_reason TEXT,\n514\t                    last_refusal_at    INTEGER,\n515\t                    comment_high_water INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT -1,\n516\t                    sla_deadline       INTEGER NOT NULL,\n517\t                    sla_flagged_at     INTEGER,\n518\t                    fenced_at          INTEGER,\n519\t                    created_at         INTEGER NOT NULL\n520\t                )\n521\t            \"\"\")\n522\t\n523\t            # Copy data from old table to new table\n524\t            # Existing rows with comment_high_water=0 are migrated as-is\n525\t            # (they represent \"comment 0 has been processed\", which is valid)\n526\t            await db.execute(\"\"\"\n527\t                INSERT INTO bead_watch_new\n528\t                SELECT * FROM bead_watch\n529\t            \"\"\")\n530\t\n531\t            # Recreate indexes\n532\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline\")\n533\t            await db.execute(\"DROP INDEX IF EXISTS idx_bead_watch_fenced\")\n534\t\n535\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_sla_deadline ON bead_watch_new(sla_deadline)\")\n536\t            await db.execute(\"CREATE INDEX idx_bead_watch_fenced ON bead_watch_new(fenced_at)\")\n537\t\n538\t            # Drop old table and rename new table\n539\t            await db.execute(\"DROP TABLE bead_watch\")\n540\t            await db.execute(\"ALTER TABLE bead_watch_new RENAME TO bead_watch\")\n541\t\n542\t            await db.commit()\n543\t            logger.info(\"Migration complete: bead_watch.comment_high_water default is now -1\")\n544\t        except Exception as e:\n545\t            await db.rollback()\n546\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to migrate bead_watch.comment_high_water: {e}\")\n547\t            raise\n548\t\n549\t    async def close(self) -> None:\n550\t        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n551\t        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n552\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n553\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n554\t\n555\t    # Session operations\n556\t    async def create_session(self, session_id: str | None = None) -> str:\n557\t        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n558\t        if not session_id:\n559\t            session_id = str(uuid4())\n560\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n561\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n562\t            await db.execute(\n563\t                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n564\t                (session_id, now, now)\n565\t            )\n566\t            await db.commit()\n567\t        return session_id\n568\t\n569\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n570\t        \"\"\"Get session by ID.\"\"\"\n571\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n572\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n573\t            async with db.execute(\n574\t                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n575\t            ) as cursor:\n576\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n577\t                return dict(row) if row else None\n578\t\n579\t    async def update_session_activity(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n580\t        \"\"\"Update session last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n581\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n582\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n583\t            await db.execute(\n584\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n585\t                (now, session_id)\n586\t            )\n587\t            await db.commit()\n588\t\n589\t    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n590\t        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n591\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n592\t            await db.execute(\n593\t                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n594\t                (surface_id, session_id)\n595\t            )\n596\t            await db.commit()\n597\t\n598\t    async def delete_session(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n599\t        \"\"\"Delete a session and every row tied to it. Returns a removal summary.\n600\t\n601\t        Used by test teardown (DELETE /api/v1/sessions/{id}) to guarantee a\n602\t        session and all its topics/results/intents/utterances/surfaces/signal\n603\t        rows are gone. Note: SQLite FK ``ON DELETE CASCADE`` is *not* enforced\n604\t        here (``PRAGMA foreign_keys`` is never enabled), so child rows are\n605\t        deleted explicitly, in dependency order, within one transaction.\n606\t\n607\t        The topics table references sessions with ``ON DELETE SET NULL``, so\n608\t        topics are *not* cascade-removed even if FKs were on — deleting them\n609\t        explicitly is required for a true clean teardown.\n610\t\n611\t        Returns ``{\"session_removed\": 0|1, \"topics_removed\": <int>}``.\n612\t        \"\"\"\n613\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n614\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n615\t\n616\t            # Count what we're about to remove (before deleting).\n617\t            async with db.execute(\n618\t                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n619\t            ) as cur:\n620\t                topics_removed = (await cur.fetchone())[0]\n621\t            async with db.execute(\n622\t                \"SELECT 1 FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n623\t            ) as cur:\n624\t                session_removed = 1 if await cur.fetchone() else 0\n625\t\n626\t            # Children first, parents last (matches FK dependency order).\n627\t            await db.execute(\n628\t                \"DELETE FROM feedback_signals WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n629\t            )\n630\t            await db.execute(\n631\t                \"DELETE FROM results WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n632\t            )\n633\t            await db.execute(\n634\t                \"DELETE FROM intent_topics WHERE intent_id IN \"\n635\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n636\t                (session_id,),\n637\t            )\n638\t            # dispatch_timings is keyed by the intent *thread* id\n639\t            # (routed_intent.intent_id), which differs from intents.id for the\n640\t            # current router path (see the table comment in SCHEMA_SQL), so this\n641\t            # is best-effort: it catches rows keyed by the store intent id, while\n642\t            # thread-keyd rows survive until the thread id is known to the caller.\n643\t            await db.execute(\n644\t                \"DELETE FROM dispatch_timings WHERE intent_id IN \"\n645\t                \"(SELECT id FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n646\t                (session_id,),\n647\t            )\n648\t            await db.execute(\n649\t                \"DELETE FROM intents WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n650\t            )\n651\t            await db.execute(\n652\t                \"DELETE FROM utterances WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n653\t            )\n654\t            await db.execute(\n655\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id IN \"\n656\t                \"(SELECT id FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?)\",\n657\t                (session_id,),\n658\t            )\n659\t            await db.execute(\n660\t                \"DELETE FROM topics WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n661\t            )\n662\t            await db.execute(\n663\t                \"DELETE FROM surfaces WHERE session_id = ?\", (session_id,)\n664\t            )\n665\t            await db.execute(\n666\t                \"DELETE FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n667\t            )\n668\t            await db.commit()\n669\t\n670\t        return {\"session_removed\": session_removed, \"topics_removed\": topics_removed}\n671\t\n672\t    # Surface operations\n673\t    async def register_surface(\n674\t        self,\n675\t        session_id: str,\n676\t        surface_type:
